articles.headline: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
articles.description: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

images.name: Rice hails England's 'frightening' attacking substitutes
images.caption: Declan Rice reacts to England's 3-0 win over Costa Rica in their final friendly ahead of the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: England sign off WC prep with 3-0 win as Rice, Gordon, Watkins score
articles.description: Returning Declan Rice set England on course for a morale-boosting 3-0 victory against Costa Rica after a heavy thunderstorm in Orlando delayed their final World Cup warmup match by an hour.

images.name: Declan Rice [1296x729]
images.caption: Declan Rice opened the scoring for England shortly after 10 minutes played.

images.name: Tuchel: England can't be World Cup favourites
images.caption: Thomas Tuchel says England can't be favourites because there are "proven winners" at the 2026 FIFA World Cup.

articles.headline: Burley questions if Portugal's Martinez would drop Ronaldo
articles.description: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

images.caption: Craig Burley debates whether Roberto Martinez should drop Cristiano Ronaldo amid growing scrutiny over his place in Portugal's starting lineup.

articles.headline: Argentina wins as Messi scores in cameo in front of 88k in Alabama
articles.description: Argentine legend Messi, who is working his way back from muscle fatigue ahead of the start of the FIFA World Cup, came on in the 70th minute and scored from the penalty spot right after in his team's 3-0 win over Iceland.

images.caption: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.
images.alt: Lionel Messi celebrates after scoring a goal for Argentina in a friendly against Iceland.

article.headline: Balard strikes on debut as Socceroos down New Zealand
article.description: Max Balard has enjoyed a perfect start to life as a Socceroo, scoring a winning goal two minutes into his Australian debut in a late 1-0 friendly win over New Zealand.

tracking.trackingName: INET_ Highlight (Balard seals 1-0 win for Socceroos on debut) 2025/09/05 AUS ONLY

article.story:
Max Balard has enjoyed a perfect start to life as a Socceroo, scoring a winning goal two minutes into his Australian debut in a late 1-0 friendly victory over New Zealand.

In their first outing since qualifying for next year's World Cup tournament in North America, a largely experimental Australian side missing most of their regular starters struggled to pose too many questions for the All Whites at Canberra's GIO Stadium.

But in the final quarter of an hour Socceroos boss Tony Popovic threw caution to the wind and injected the youthful energy of Nestory Irankunda, Mohamed Toure and Balard into the action.

Their introductions sparked Australia into life and Toure was the man who set up Balard for the 87th minute winner.

"Nestory made an impact, Mo made an impact and then later Maxy scores a wonderful goal," Popovic said.

"I went to congratulate all the players and he (Balard) was, obviously ecstatic, jubilant.

"I gave him a hug and said, 'Congratulations, it's a very proud moment, don't forget it and try and get more'."

Balard, a former Central Coast Mariners midfielder now on the books of Dutch side NAC Breda, had only been brought on for his first cap two minutes earlier.

The win will give Popovic plenty of food for thought ahead of next year's World Cup, with Jordan Bos a particular standout for much of Friday's game.

"We haven't seen him since November, unfortunately and that was his first full game with me," Popovic said.

"He hasn't played a lot with us but from what I saw in November to now, he's a much improved player and that's a real positive going forward."

Injury and the form of Aziz Behich have largely prevented the Feyenoord defender from getting regular game time under Popovic but the Melburnian looked instantly assured as the starting left-sided wingback.

Bos' marauding runs forward made New Zealand appear vulnerable and on one foray upfield he slalomed past three All Whites before sending a dipping shot just over the bar.

While Bos was having joy at one end, Chris Wood was causing problems for the Australian defence as the Nottingham Forest forward bullied Milos Degenek off the ball in the early stages.

Twice the powerful Kiwi forward lashed efforts at the Australian goal but both times debutant goalkeeper Paul Izzo stood up to the test.

Izzo was barely troubled in the second half and the same could be said for New Zealand counterpart Max Crocombe.

That was until Irankunda and Toure were thrown on with 15 minutes to go.

Watford winger Irankunda received one of the biggest cheers of the night when he was introduced and drew a foul with his first touch.

He tried to guide his free-kick over the New Zealand wall and then glanced a header over the bar.

But Toure was the man to eventually provide Australia with the breakthrough when he threaded a delightful ball in to Balard.

The 24-year-old Balard struck low and beyond Crocombe's reach into the back of the net to extend Popovic's unbeaten start as Socceroos boss to nine games.

videos.headline: Balard seals 1-0 win for Socceroos on debut
videos.description: Max Balard scores a late winner for the Socceroos in a dream debut.
